compiled_state.title: completion-verifier: story-level FAIL when task description has narrower scope than story DDs
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: /dso:sprint session (2026-04-23), story fd0f-c05d

## Observed
Task 43cd-465b was created with a narrower scope (3 files) than story fd0f-c05d's done definitions (5 criteria across 4 files + downstream scan). The completion-verifier correctly returned FAIL, requiring a follow-up task 3696-696f to cover the remaining gaps.

## Root cause
Task descriptions are written by preplanning or manually, and may not fully cover all story DDs. The completion-verifier reads the story's done definitions verbatim and compares against implementation — it does not limit itself to the task's acceptance criteria summary.

## Impact
Extra sprint cycle (create follow-up task → implement → re-verify). Adds overhead to stories where implementation-plan generated tasks underspecify the scope.

## Expected
Either (a) implementation-plan ensures task acceptance criteria fully covers story DDs, or (b) sprint flags the scope gap before task dispatch so the task description can be corrected proactively.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: (1) ticket describes completion-verifier FAIL due to implementation-plan task underspecification (guidance gap), (2) fix requires implementation-plan/preplanning SKILL.md update.
comments.author: Worktree
